purpose: Machine-readable bundle map that links public runtime guarantees to scoped proof lanes, stale/missing evidence fixtures, source anchors, and no-claim boundaries.

bundle_schema.claim_text: human-facing claim, scoped to the listed lanes
bundle_schema.semantic_scope: what part of the runtime or public API the bundle is allowed to discuss
bundle_schema.primary_lanes: proof manifest lane IDs that carry the strongest evidence
bundle_schema.supporting_lanes: manifest or governance lanes that support freshness and routing
bundle_schema.freshness_policy: rules for when cached or stale evidence cannot be cited
bundle_schema.failure_mode_examples: fixtures that demonstrate the contract fails closed
bundle_schema.stale_missing_fixtures: fixture IDs covering stale, missing, or local-fallback evidence
bundle_schema.source_paths: repo paths that must exist for the bundle to stay fresh
bundle_schema.docs_links: operator or user docs that explain the guarantee
bundle_schema.no_claims: explicit statements of what this bundle does not prove

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: Tasks are claimed only through structured ownership evidence; cite this bundle for no-orphan-task claims only when the formal and validation-frontier evidence are fresh.
failure_mode_examples: orphan task fixture without owning region
closed region with live child task
cached formal build cited after source overlap
no_claims: does not prove every adapter, protocol implementation, backend, or distributed transport path
does not prove release readiness or broad workspace health

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: Race losers are discussed only through formal invariant and semantic-lint evidence; this bundle does not itself prove every combinator call site.
semantic_scope: semantic lint detection of drop-based loser handling
failure_mode_examples: semantic lint summary missing drop-race-loser rule
manifest lane exists but stale receipt is cited as fresh
no_claims: does not prove performance or fairness of every race caller
does not replace focused combinator tests for newly changed code

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: No-obligation-leak claims require obligation-ledger source anchors plus fresh formal, semantic-lint, or validation-frontier evidence.
failure_mode_examples: permit reserved then neither committed nor aborted
panic-leak tracker cited without source freshness
semantic-lint proof lane missing ASUP diagnostic mapping
no_claims: does not prove new obligation code without rerunning affected lanes
does not authorize closing leak bugs from cached evidence alone

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: Cancel-safe send claims route through channel public-run evidence and the MPSC/select validation frontier; broad channel correctness still needs focused tests for changed surfaces.
failure_mode_examples: SelectAll loser future keeps receiver borrowed
public-run binary cited after channel source overlap
no_claims: does not prove broadcast, watch, oneshot, or every future channel type
does not prove throughput, fairness, or conformance crate health

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: Deterministic replay claims are scoped to lab snapshot/replay anchors and validation-frontier evidence, not arbitrary production replay success.
failure_mode_examples: stale validation-frontier receipt cited as fresh
no_claims: does not prove arbitrary production traces can always replay
does not prove crashpack durability, performance, or release readiness

public_guarantee_bundles.claim_text: The default production no-tokio claim is citeable only through the default-production-tokio-tree lane and its feature-boundary source anchors.
semantic_scope: README and AGENTS no-tokio claim routing
failure_mode_examples: tokio appears in default normal dependency graph
workspace graph cited as default-production proof
fuzz quarantine path cited as production no-tokio evidence
no_claims: does not cover fuzz, workspace dev-dependencies, conformance, or asupersync-tokio-compat
does not prove optional metrics or future feature combinations unless their own lanes are fresh

stale_missing_fixture_cases.reason: A bundle without at least one manifest-backed primary lane cannot support a public guarantee.
A stale RCH receipt cannot be cited as fresh semantic evidence.
Local Cargo fallback is not evidence for remote-required proof lanes.
Dirty source overlap with a bundle source path requires rerunning the affected lane.
Evidence bundles must state what they do not prove.

golden_report.summary: Every public guarantee row has at least one primary proof lane, a freshness policy, failure examples, fixture coverage, and explicit no-claim boundaries.

rows.next_action: rerun formal-lean-build and validation-frontier-final-signoff before broad citation
rerun formal and semantic-lint lanes after race/combinator source overlap
rerun obligation-relevant formal, semantic-lint, or validation-frontier lanes
rerun channel-mpsc-select-e2e-public-run after channel source overlap
rerun default-production-tokio-tree after dependency or feature changes

validation.rch_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_semantic_evidence_bundles_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test semantic_evidence_bundles_contract --no-default-features -- --nocapture

no_claims: This artifact does not execute any proof lane by itself.
This artifact does not prove broad workspace health, release readiness, runtime correctness outside cited scopes, performance improvement, or live RCH fleet availability.
This artifact does not turn cached, stale, failed, local-fallback, or advisory evidence into fresh proof.
This artifact does not authorize deleting files, creating branches, creating worktrees, or closing source bugs without focused validation.
